mitigation.title: Fix config file permissions
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by tightening DinTalClaw config file permissions and reducing local read exposure.

form_schema.label: Fix file permissions using ACL or chmod 600

mitigation.title: Fix memory directory permissions
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by restricting permissions on the memory directory so runtime memory data is less exposed.

form_schema.label: Fix directory permissions using ACL or chmod 700

mitigation.title: Stop running DinTalClaw as root
mitigation.description: This risk requires manual remediation and should only display operational guidance. Move the service to a restricted non root account.

items.action: Restart the service as a non root user
items.detail: Stop the current root owned process and relaunch DinTalClaw with a dedicated low privilege account.

items.action: Review startup scripts and service units
items.detail: Verify that systemd units, container entry points, or shell scripts do not explicitly launch DinTalClaw as root.
Grant the runtime account only the minimum write access needed for config, logs, and working directories.

mitigation.title: Fix log directory permissions
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by restricting permissions on the log directory to reduce unauthorized read, delete, or tamper access.

form_schema.label: Fix directory permissions using ACL or chmod 700

mitigation.title: Scan unreviewed skills
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by running the skill security scan for installed DinTalClaw skills.

mitigation.title: Remediate risky skill
mitigation.description: This risk can be fixed automatically by applying a skill remediation action such as deletion when the skill is not trusted.
